sections.title: Market Pulse: Mixed Signals Amid Geopolitical Tensions

items.headline: U.S. Futures Mixed Early, Tech Leads Gains Despite Escalating Middle East Conflict
items.summary: U.S. stock futures showed mixed movements early Thursday, July 9, 2026, with the Nasdaq leading gains, primarily driven by strength in technology and artificial intelligence (AI) stocks. This comes as investors weigh intensifying geopolitical tensions in the Middle East following new U.S. airstrikes against Iran and retaliatory actions. Oil prices, after surging on Wednesday, saw slight declines or steady trading early Thursday. The overall market sentiment remains cautious, although the resilience of the tech sector is providing some support. Not financial advice.

items.statContext: Up 0.06% on Thursday (July 9, 2026), after closing Wednesday at 7,482.71 (-0.28%).
Indicating a positive open on Thursday, after closing Wednesday higher by 0.20%.
Indicating a mixed to slightly negative open on Thursday, after closing Wednesday at 52,348.39 (-1.09%).

items.headline: Micron Technology Surges on Strong Q2 Earnings Contribution
items.summary: Micron Technology ($MU) saw its shares rise 7.2%, emerging as the strongest upward force on the S&P 500. The memory-chip maker is reported to be the largest contributor to S&P 500 Q2 earnings growth, with earnings soaring over 1,200%.

items.headline: Penguin Solutions Beats Q3 Earnings Estimates, Stock Jumps
items.summary: Penguin Solutions Inc. ($PENG) shares soared 25.1% after the company reported third-quarter fiscal 2026 adjusted earnings of $0.84 per share, surpassing the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $0.63 per share.

items.headline: Alibaba Shows Strong Monthly Gains
items.summary: Alibaba Group Holding Limited ($BABA) has recorded a significant monthly gain of +11.22% as of July 9, 2026, indicating sustained positive momentum.

items.headline: Occidental Petroleum Rises Amid Oil Market Volatility
items.summary: Occidental Petroleum Corporation ($OXY) has seen a monthly gain of +11.79% as of July 9, 2026, benefiting from the recent volatility and upward pressure on oil prices due to Middle East tensions.

items.headline: PepsiCo Slips Despite Q2 Revenue Beat on North American Weakness
items.summary: PepsiCo ($PEP) shares fell 4.8% on Thursday, after ticking down 1% earlier, despite reporting stronger-than-expected second-quarter revenue. The decline is attributed to weaker demand and tightening consumer budgets in North America.

items.headline: Elicio Therapeutics Experiences Significant Decline Post-Offering
items.summary: Elicio Therapeutics, Inc. ($ELTX) has recorded a substantial monthly decline of -37.35% in July, following the announcement of a $15 million registered direct offering.

items.headline: U.S.-Iran Conflict Escalates, Driving Market Caution and Oil Price Volatility
items.summary: Renewed U.S. airstrikes against Iran and Tehran's retaliatory actions targeting Gulf countries have significantly escalated Middle East tensions. This geopolitical development is a primary driver of current market caution, leading to volatility in oil prices and broader equity market uncertainty. President Donald Trump's remarks casting doubt on a temporary ceasefire further amplify the instability.

items.headline: Oil Prices Fluctuate as Middle East Tensions Persist
items.summary: Brent crude, the international standard, surged over 5% on Wednesday, briefly topping $80 per barrel, but later inched up 64 cents to $78.66 on Thursday before slipping to $77.94. Benchmark U.S. crude also saw gains then declines, reflecting market sensitivity to the ongoing Middle East conflict and its potential impact on supply routes like the Strait of Hormuz. Gasoline prices have also begun to climb.

items.headline: Tech and AI Sectors Lead Gains; Financial and Realty Show Strength Globally
items.summary: The technology, semiconductors, and AI-linked investment companies are providing a significant positive contribution to global markets. In India, the Realty, Media, and Capital Markets sectors were notable leaders, with the Nifty Realty index up 2.92%. Conversely, the Information Technology and Industrials Select Sector SPDRs saw declines on Wednesday.

items.headline: Jobless Claims Remain Steady; Fed Rate Hike Probability Rises
items.summary: Weekly Initial Jobless Claims came in at 215,000, consistent with the previous week, while Continuing Claims held at 1.814 million. Meanwhile, the CME FedWatch Tool indicates a significantly higher probability for at least one Federal Reserve rate increase before the end of 2026, as hawkish Fed minutes from June's meeting continue to influence expectations. Fed officials are scheduled to speak later today.

items.headline: Q2 Earnings Season Kicks Off with PepsiCo Report, Micron Leads S&P 500 Growth
items.summary: PepsiCo ($PEP) unofficially commenced the Q2 earnings season, beating estimates for both earnings and revenue, though its stock dipped due to softer North American sales. Micron Technology ($MU) has emerged as the largest contributor to S&P 500 Q2 earnings growth, with earnings soaring over 1,200%. The broader earnings season is set to intensify next week with major banks and airlines reporting.
